platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: **Microsoft Graph API: New authentication protocol types added** — `nativeAuth`, `authenticationTransfer`, `samlOnBehalfOf`, `implicitAccessTokenAndGetResponseMode`, `authorizationCodeWithPkce`, `clientCredentials`, `refreshTokenGrant` now explicitly surfaced in the Graph API changelog. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

**Microsoft Graph API: `microsoftRevokedSessions` risk detail member** — New enumeration member added to `riskDetail` for risk detection scenarios. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/
**Microsoft Graph API: `webCategory` resource type** — New resource for network filtering and compliance classification. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

**Microsoft Graph API: `emailSettings` resource type** — New resource added to Lifecycle Workflows, with `emailSettings` property on `lifecycleManagementSettings`. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

**Microsoft Graph API: eDiscovery resources surfaced** — `ediscoveryCase`, `reviewSet`, `reviewSetQuery` resources now explicitly listed in the compliance/Bicep section. Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **AWS Agent Registry (April 9, 2026)** — New centralized catalog service for discovering and managing agents, MCP servers, tools, and skills. Provides governed workflows for publishing, reviewing, and approving resources with hybrid semantic and keyword search. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

**Google ADK (Google Agent Development Kit) framework support** — Added to AgentCore Runtime integrations alongside CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Agents SDK, and Strands Agents. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html

**API surface expanded to 43 actions** (up from 38) — New API operations added consistent with the Registry and other services. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/APIReference/API_Operations.html

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: **Mythos preview model listed in product lineup** — New model added alongside Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku. Gated research preview for defensive cybersecurity work as part of Project Glasswing. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-mythos-preview-risk-report

**Microsoft Foundry added as integration platform** — Claude now available through Microsoft Foundry in addition to Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud Vertex AI. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-in-microsoft-foundry

**Word add-in now available** — Microsoft Word extension added alongside existing Excel, PowerPoint, and Slack integrations. Source: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/14465370-use-claude-for-word

findings.title: AWS Agent Registry in Bedrock AgentCore
findings.description: New centralized catalog service (launched April 9, 2026) for discovering and managing agents, MCP servers, tools, and skills within AgentCore. Features governed publishing/approval workflows and hybrid semantic+keyword search. Introduces new inventory entities: registry records, agent entries, MCP server entries, tool entries, skill entries, and approval workflows. AISPM must discover and catalog all resources published to the Registry, map their approval states, and flag ungoverned or unapproved agents

findings.title: Google ADK Framework Support in Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
findings.description: Google Agent Development Kit added as a supported framework for AgentCore Runtime, joining CrewAI, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Agents SDK, and Strands Agents. Agents built with Google ADK can now be deployed and executed on AgentCore infrastructure. AISPM framework detection must be updated to identify and inventory Google ADK-based agents running in AgentCore

findings.title: Anthropic Mythos Preview Model
findings.description: New "Mythos preview" model added to Anthropic's product lineup as a gated research preview for defensive cybersecurity work under Project Glasswing. Strikingly capable at computer security tasks — emerging capability not explicitly trained. AISPM should track Mythos model deployments and ensure governance policies cover this new model, particularly given its dual-use security capabilities

findings.title: Anthropic Mythos Preview — Exploit Generation Capability
findings.description: Mythos Preview demonstrates a step-change in autonomous exploit generation. Anthropic reports it developed working JavaScript engine exploits 181 times vs. Opus 4.6's 2, with register control achieved 29 more times. This dramatically expands the automated red teaming attack surface — AIRT must update threat models to account for Mythos-class exploit capability across all monitored platforms

findings.title: Claude Available via Microsoft Foundry
findings.description: Microsoft Foundry added as a new deployment platform for Claude models (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6 in East US 2 and Sweden Central), alongside Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud Vertex AI. This creates a new discovery surface — AISPM must now scan Microsoft Foundry for Claude model deployments in addition to existing platforms

findings.title: Claude Word Add-in
findings.description: Microsoft Word add-in now available for Claude, completing the Office integration surface (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Slack). Enterprise users can draft, edit, and revise documents with Claude from a persistent sidebar, with edits appearing as tracked changes. Endpoint monitoring should track data flows between Word documents and Claude to detect sensitive document content exposure

findings.title: Microsoft Graph Auth Protocol and Risk Detection Expansion
findings.description: New authentication protocol types (`nativeAuth`, `authenticationTransfer`, `samlOnBehalfOf`) added to Microsoft Graph API, expanding authentication pathways for Copilot and M365 integrations. Additionally, `microsoftRevokedSessions` added as a new `riskDetail` enumeration member, and `webCategory` resource type added for network compliance. AISPM should update permission and authentication monitoring to cover these new auth flows and risk signals

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **OpenAI Codex CLI v0.120.0 released** (Apr 11): MCP Apps gain richer functionality including resource reads and tool metadata. Remote workflows now support egress websocket transport with sandbox-aware filesystem APIs. WebRTC-based voice sessions become the default transport option. New resume feature for jumping directly to sessions by ID or name. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog

**OpenAI Codex CLI v0.119.0 released** (Apr 10): Continued enhancements to the Codex agent platform including Realtime V2 streaming of background agent progress. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog

findings.title: OpenAI Codex MCP Apps Resource Reads and Tool Metadata
findings.description: Codex v0.120.0 expands MCP Apps to support resource reads and tool metadata access, beyond just tool invocation. This means MCP Apps running inside Codex can now pull data from MCP servers (reading resources) and inspect tool schemas (metadata), expanding the data access surface area. Endpoint monitoring should track what resources MCP Apps read and what tool metadata they access, as these represent new data flow paths from enterprise MCP servers into the Codex environment

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Remote Workflow Egress WebSocket Transport
findings.description: Codex v0.120.0 introduces egress websocket transport for remote workflows with sandbox-aware filesystem APIs. This creates a new outbound data flow pattern — Codex agents can now establish persistent websocket connections from their sandbox to external endpoints, with filesystem access within the sandbox boundary. AIDR should monitor these outbound websocket connections as potential data exfiltration vectors

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **Agent Registry service launched** (Apr 9): New centralized catalog for discovering and managing agents, MCP servers, tools, and skills within AgentCore. Provides governed workflows for publishing, reviewing, and approving resources with hybrid semantic and keyword search. Registry is now listed as a core AgentCore service alongside Runtime, Memory, Gateway, Identity, Code Interpreter, Browser, Observability, Evaluations, and Policy. New API action: `SearchRegistryRecords`. Can be accessed via console, APIs, or as an MCP server directly from IDEs. Available in preview in 5 AWS regions. Source: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-agent-registry-in-agentcore-preview/

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **`web_search_call.results` added to ResponseIncludable** (Apr 8, Python SDK v2.31.0): Web search call results can now be included as a response includable type, expanding the data returned in API responses. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/changelog

**`phase` field added to Conversations message type** (Apr 8, SDK v2.31.0 / v6.34.0): New field on conversation messages across both Python and Node SDKs. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/changelog

**Short-lived token authentication** (Apr 8, SDK v2.31.0 / v6.34.0): New client authentication mechanism using short-lived tokens, available in both Python and Node SDKs. Source: https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/build/auth
**Conversations API response compaction** (community discussion): New capability for managing conversation length limits via response compaction. Source: https://community.openai.com

platforms.name: Salesforce AgentForce
details: **Agent "Topics" renamed to "Subagents"** (Apr 2026): Beginning April 2026, Salesforce has renamed agent "topics" to "subagents" across all AgentForce and Einstein GenAI documentation. Salesforce states this is a terminology/concept realignment with no functional changes. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/blog/agentforce-guide/

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Bugbot MCP Support** (Apr 8): Bugbot code review agent now has access to MCP servers, giving it full tool-use context during code reviews rather than just static analysis. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog/04-08-26

**Bugbot Learned Rules** (Apr 8): Bugbot now self-improves by turning user feedback signals into learned rules that shape future code reviews. New entity type: learned rules. 110K+ repos enabled, 44K+ rules generated. Source: https://cursor.com/blog/bugbot-learning
**Bugbot "Fix All" action** (Apr 8): Users can apply all Bugbot-suggested fixes in a single operation. Source: https://cursor.com/changelog/04-08-26

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **Cross-device Sync** (Apr 9, v1.26.0): Dia launched browser data synchronization across multiple devices using 6-character pairing codes. Data is end-to-end encrypted. Includes recovery kit creation. This is a major new capability for a previously local-first browser. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

findings.title: AWS Bedrock AgentCore Agent Registry
findings.description: New centralized catalog service for discovering and managing agents, MCP servers, tools, and skills. Introduces new inventory entities: registry records, agent listings, MCP server listings, tool listings, and skill listings with governed publish/review/approve workflows. New API action `SearchRegistryRecords` provides hybrid semantic and keyword search across registry contents. The registry is also accessible as an MCP server from IDEs. AISPM must discover, inventory, and map all registry resources and their relationships

findings.title: OpenAI web_search_call.results ResponseIncludable
findings.description: The OpenAI Responses API now supports `web_search_call.results` as an includable type, meaning web search results can be embedded directly in API responses. This creates a new data flow surface — external web content flowing through the API into enterprise applications. AIDR should monitor these results for data exfiltration and content injection risks

findings.title: OpenAI Short-lived Token Authentication
findings.description: New authentication mechanism using short-lived tokens for OpenAI API clients, replacing long-lived API keys in cloud-managed environments. Changes how sessions are established and authenticated, affecting identity tracking and session monitoring. AIDR must recognize this auth pattern to maintain audit trail coverage

findings.title: Salesforce AgentForce Subagents entity model change
findings.description: Salesforce renamed agent "topics" to "subagents" across AgentForce and Einstein GenAI. While described as a terminology change, this signals a conceptual shift in how Salesforce models agent composition — subagents are now positioned as specialized agents with specific expertise within a parent agent. AISPM inventory must update entity type mappings from "topics" to "subagents" to maintain accurate discovery and governance of Salesforce agent structures

findings.title: Cursor Bugbot MCP Server Access
findings.description: Cursor's Bugbot code review agent now connects to MCP servers for full tool-use context during reviews. This expands the attack surface — a compromised or malicious MCP server could influence code review outcomes, and Bugbot now has access to tools and data beyond the code itself. Endpoint monitoring should track Bugbot's MCP connections and tool invocations

findings.title: Cursor Bugbot Learned Rules
findings.description: Bugbot now self-improves by converting feedback into persistent learned rules that shape future code reviews (110K+ repos, 44K+ rules). This creates a new prompt injection / rule poisoning vector — adversarial feedback could train Bugbot to ignore security issues or approve malicious patterns. AIRT should evaluate rule poisoning as an attack surface

platforms.name: Claude Enterprise (Anthropic)
details: **Project Glasswing** (Apr 7): Multi-company initiative to enhance global software security. Participating organizations include AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks. This is a policy/partnership announcement, not a new product or API. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news

**Google & Broadcom infrastructure partnership** (Apr 6): Anthropic secured multiple gigawatts of next-generation computing infrastructure through expanded Google and Broadcom partnership. Infrastructure expansion — no new services. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Codex model availability update** (Apr 7): Older models (gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-mini/max, gpt-5.1, gpt-5) removed from model picker. Remaining: gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2. Pro subscribers also get gpt-5.3-codex-spark. This is model lifecycle housekeeping, not a new service. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **Adaptive model bug fix** (Apr 7, v1.9600.41): Fixed model switching issue introduced with the Adaptive feature. Bug fix only. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: **Agent evaluations GA**: Agent evaluations moved from preview to general availability. Validates agent performance using customizable test sets across diverse scenarios. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/analytics-agent-evaluation-intro

**Multi-turn conversation tests**: New capability to create multi-turn conversation tests to evaluate agents across realistic dialog flows instead of single-turn interactions. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/analytics-agent-evaluation-multi-turn

**ChatGPT-5 GA globally**: ChatGPT-5 is now generally available globally (excluding GCC) for use in production agents. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/authoring-select-agent-model

**Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus GA globally**: Third-party Anthropic models now generally available globally (excluding GCC) for production agents, enabling fine-grained control over reasoning depth, quality, latency, and cost. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/authoring-select-agent-model

**Agent node for agent flows**: New capability to call a Copilot Studio agent from an agent flow, enabling agent-to-agent orchestration. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/agent-node-workflow

**Bing Custom Search as knowledge source**: Agents can now use Bing Custom Search to ground responses in a curated, scoped web index using a Custom Configuration ID. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/knowledge-bing-custom-search

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Metadata Search for RAG Engine** (April 6): Schema-based metadata filtering capability added to Vertex AI RAG Engine. Users can define metadata schemas for corpora, attach metadata to files, and filter contexts during retrieval operations. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/rag-engine/use-metadata-search

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **Adaptive Model Router** (April 6): New model routing feature that intelligently selects the best model for each task. Available to Pro, Max, and Teams plan users. Dynamically chooses underlying model while maintaining fixed per-token quota consumption. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

**Updated Model Picker with Pricing Context** (April 6): Token pricing display showing input, output, and cache read rates per model; prompt cache timer; token counts in response cards. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

findings.title: Copilot Studio Agent Node (Agent-to-Agent Orchestration)
findings.description: New agent node allows calling a Copilot Studio agent from an agent flow, enabling multi-agent orchestration chains. Introduces new entity relationships: agent flow → agent invocations. AISPM must map these agent-to-agent call chains to detect toxic combinations such as overprivileged agents invoking other agents or unmonitored delegation paths

findings.title: Copilot Studio Bing Custom Search Knowledge Source
findings.description: Agents can now connect to Bing Custom Search as a knowledge source via Custom Configuration ID. Introduces new knowledge connection type: curated web index. AISPM must discover and inventory these connections and monitor for data exposure through externally-sourced web content flowing into agent responses

findings.title: Copilot Studio Voice Post-Call Actions
findings.description: Voice-enabled agents can now trigger automated backend actions after call completion. Introduces new entity: post-call action topics with automatic execution triggers. AISPM must track these automated action configurations to detect unmonitored backend operations triggered by voice agent conversations

findings.title: Third-Party Models GA in Copilot Studio (ChatGPT-5, Claude Opus/Sonnet)
findings.description: ChatGPT-5 and multiple Anthropic Claude models (Sonnet 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus) are now generally available globally for production agents. AISPM must track which external models production agents use, as model choice affects data processing boundaries — some models may require data processing outside the organization's geographic boundaries

findings.title: Copilot Studio Multi-Turn Conversation Evaluation Tests
findings.description: New multi-turn conversation test capability for evaluating agents across realistic dialog flows. Agent evaluations are now GA. Introduces new data artifacts: test sets, evaluation results, and multi-turn conversation test configurations. AIDR should monitor evaluation data flows as they may contain sensitive conversation patterns

findings.title: Vertex AI RAG Engine Metadata Search
findings.description: New schema-based metadata filtering for RAG Engine corpora. Users can define metadata schemas, attach metadata to files, and filter contexts during retrieval. Extends RAG data flow surface with structured metadata that controls what knowledge is retrieved. AISPM should track metadata schema configurations as they influence agent knowledge access boundaries

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **RAG Engine Serverless Mode** (April 3, public preview): Fully managed database mode for RAG Engine that abstracts away database provisioning and scaling. Introduces new `RagManagedVertexVectorSearch` vector database type. Supports seamless transition between Serverless and Spanner deployment modes. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/rag-engine/serverless-mode

**Veo 3.1 Lite** (April 2, public preview): Most cost-efficient Veo video generation model on Vertex AI. Completes the Veo 3.1 family (standard, Fast, Lite) — all with native audio generation. Source: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/veo-3-1-lite-and-a-new-veo-upscaling-capability-on-vertex-ai

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Codex Plugin for Claude Code** (April 4): Official OpenAI MCP integration (`openai/codex-plugin-cc`) that allows Claude Code users to delegate code review, adversarial review, and background tasks to OpenAI Codex. Creates cross-provider data flow via Model Context Protocol. Source: https://github.com/openai/codex-plugin-cc

**Codex Pay-as-You-Go Team Seats** (April 2): ChatGPT Business and Enterprise workspaces can now add Codex-only seats with usage-based pricing (no fixed seat fee). Introduces a new user seat type within enterprise workspaces. Source: https://openai.com/index/codex-flexible-pricing-for-teams/

**ChatGPT in Apple CarPlay** (April 4, all plans): Voice-only ChatGPT access via Apple CarPlay on iOS 26.4+. Available on all plans including enterprise. New access surface for enterprise conversations. Source: https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001153-using-chatgpt-on-carplay

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Cursor 3 Interface** (April 2): Major release introducing three new capabilities:
**Agents Window**: Standalone workspace for running parallel agents across local, cloud, and remote SSH environments
**Design Mode**: Annotate and target UI elements directly in browsers for agent feedback
**Agent Tabs**: Side-by-side or grid view of multiple agent sessions
New `/worktree` command for isolated git worktrees, `/best-of-n` for parallel multi-model comparison, `Await` tool for monitoring long-running jobs, structured MCP content support
Source: https://cursor.com/changelog/3-0

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **v1.25.0** (April 2): Split-screen multitasking (drag tab to screen edge), custom tab icons (emoji favicons), Spotify integration visual (animated notes, hover for current track), download guidance animations. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

findings.title: Vertex AI RAG Engine Serverless Mode
findings.description: New fully managed deployment mode for RAG Engine in public preview. Introduces `RagManagedVertexVectorSearch` as a new vector database entity type. AISPM should discover RAG Engine deployment mode configurations and map Serverless vs Spanner resource types to track which mode tenant data resides in

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Plugin for Claude Code (MCP)
findings.description: Official cross-provider MCP integration allowing Claude Code to route code review and task delegation to OpenAI Codex. Creates a new enterprise data flow where source code and review context traverse from Anthropic's agent to OpenAI's Codex. Endpoint monitoring must detect this MCP server installation and flag the cross-provider data exfiltration surface

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Pay-as-You-Go Team Seats
findings.description: New Codex-only seat type in ChatGPT Business/Enterprise workspaces with usage-based billing. Introduces a new user entity type (Codex-only member) that may have different permissions and access patterns than standard seats. AISPM inventory should distinguish Codex-only seats from full ChatGPT seats when mapping workspace members

findings.title: ChatGPT CarPlay Voice Access
findings.description: ChatGPT now accessible via Apple CarPlay on all plans including enterprise. Creates a new unmonitored access surface — voice conversations in vehicles bypass typical enterprise DLP controls. AIDR should flag CarPlay as an activity source for enterprise session tracking

findings.title: Cursor 3 Agents Window and Parallel Execution
findings.description: Cursor 3 introduces a fundamentally new agent execution model — the Agents Window supports parallel agent execution across local, cloud, and remote SSH environments simultaneously. Design Mode adds browser-annotation capabilities. The `/worktree` command creates isolated git worktrees, and the `Await` tool monitors long-running subagents. Endpoint monitoring must account for parallel agent sessions, cloud-local handoff, and the expanded MCP structured content surface
